title: NCC Seeks Stakeholder Input on Regulatory Framework for A2P Messaging - FinancialEDGE
description: The Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) has convened a stakeholder forum to discuss the proposed regulatory framework for Application-to-Person (A2P) messaging. html:
The Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) has convened a stakeholder forum to discuss the proposed regulatory framework for Application-to-Person (A2P) messaging.

The forum aims to address challenges in the international A2P messaging space in Nigeria, including consumer protection, fraud prevention, and ensuring industry fairness.

A2P messaging plays a vital role in today’s digital world, enabling businesses to send notifications directly to consumers. However, the ecosystem faces significant challenges, including fraud, spam, data privacy concerns, and unequal distribution of value.

The proposed regulatory framework seeks to create a sustainable and thriving A2P messaging ecosystem that protects consumers, promotes fair competition, and unlocks new opportunities for growth. Key objectives include:

– *Centralizing International A2P Message Routing*: To ensure efficient and secure message routing
– *Mandating Compliance with a Unified Platform*: To promote transparency and fairness in the ecosystem
– *Protecting User Data with Stringent Security Protocols*: To safeguard consumers’ sensitive information
– *Fostering a Competitive and Transparent Market*: To encourage innovation and fair competition among players

The NCC emphasizes the importance of inclusivity and collaboration in effective regulation, urging stakeholders to engage constructively and provide feedback to refine the framework.
